description: UE5 built-in damage framework: ApplyDamage, TakeDamage, DamageTypes, radial damage, and point damage

entries.title: Damage Framework Overview
entries.description: UE5 has a built-in damage system centered around UGameplayStatics::ApplyDamage() and AActor::TakeDamage(). Any actor can deal and receive damage without custom code — just override TakeDamage or bind OnTakeAnyDamage.
entries.whatItIs: The UE5 Damage Framework is a built-in system where damage is applied via UGameplayStatics helper functions and received via AActor::TakeDamage() or damage delegates. It supports flat damage, directional point damage, and radial area damage, all with optional damage type classification.
whenToUse: Any game that needs health/damage mechanics — shooters, RPGs, action games, tower defense
When you want a standardized damage pipeline that all actors can participate in without custom interfaces
When you need radial/explosion damage with falloff out of the box
Prototyping combat systems quickly before deciding if you need GAS (Gameplay Ability System)
whenNotToUse: When using the Gameplay Ability System (GAS) — GAS has its own damage pipeline via Gameplay Effects that replaces direct ApplyDamage calls
Highly complex damage calculations with stacking buffs, resistances, and status effects — consider GAS or a custom system
Non-combat games where no actors take damage
When you need networked damage prediction and rollback — the built-in system has no replication logic; you must add it
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call Super::TakeDamage() when overriding — this breaks the delegate-based damage events (OnTakeAnyDamage, etc.)
Passing nullptr for InstigatorController — this makes it impossible to track kill credit or damage sources
Not setting up collision channels properly for radial damage — ApplyRadialDamage uses a trace and will be blocked by walls if you want it to be
Applying damage in the constructor or before BeginPlay — actors are not fully initialized yet
Using ApplyDamage on actors that do not override TakeDamage or bind OnTakeAnyDamage, then wondering why nothing happens — the default TakeDamage does nothing visible
keyPoints: ApplyDamage(Actor, Damage, InstigatorController, DamageCauser, DamageType): simple flat damage
ApplyPointDamage(): adds hit location and direction (for directional hit reactions)
ApplyRadialDamage(): area damage at a point, with falloff. For explosions, grenades.
ApplyRadialDamageWithFalloff(): radial damage with inner/outer radius and falloff curve
TakeDamage(): virtual function on AActor. Override for custom damage handling.
OnTakeAnyDamage: delegate on all Actors — bind without overriding TakeDamage
OnTakePointDamage: delegate for directional damage (includes hit info)
UDamageType: base class for damage categories. Subclass for Fire, Ice, Physical, etc.
InstigatorController: the player/AI that caused the damage (for kill credit)
DamageCauser: the actor that directly dealt the damage (projectile, trap, etc.)
